F1. 386 () @ 28 33, 1993, WAL 422( ) Al -- 2 of 4 -- [P I—-TvE 3()] IR ST 79 ¢ SRR 3 19 7,193, TAHLEL 801(3) AHrE31 Raww, 1993, Wwifi. 176( %) aid@ 3 s, 1996, T 631(31) ARG 31 SR 1997, WAL 504() TG 20 3, 1998, R WAL 7() o 2 TG, 1999, L 682(3) T 5 SRR, 1999, MwLFL 742(3n) i 25 e, 2000, WS 72() A 6 TR, 2001, WL 54( ) A, 22 Sl 2002, IHLEL 371(3N) TG 17 7, 2002, THLF. 489(30) e 9 e 2002, TN, 1088 () A 11 SRR, 2002 K WA 849(31) aE@ 30 Feww, 2002, L 52003) THIE | A, 2003, Fef 02(a7) wilre 29 T 2004, MmALFL 448() W 12 Y, 2004, RETH TLHLFL. 520 (1) WG 12 301, 2004, WLt 272 (1) Al 5 3, 2005, WMFLFL 315(30) e 16 T, 2005, T 546() @ 30 3%, 2005, MFHLFL 46(37) TR 3 wed, 2006, WAL 464() W@, 7 M, 2006 AR WA 566 () W@ 29 I, 2007, WHLFL 704 (F) AW 12 R, 2007 UELFL 186(3F), T 18 A, 2008, WL, 280( <), AT u atier, 2008 S WL 344 (1), arlte 7 =, 2008 R0 A VEAREA WO FHY T | MINISTRY OFENVIRONMENT AND FORESTS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 30th May, 2008 G.S.R. 414(E).—n exercise of the powers conferred by Sections 6 and 25 of the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 (29 of 1986 ), the Central Government hereby makes the following Rules further to amend the Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986, namely :— L (1) These rules may be called the Environment (Protection) Fourth Amendment Rules, 2008. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Inthe Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986,— () inschedule I, after serial number 98 and the entries relating thereto, the following serial number and entries shall be inserted, namely :-— 31.No. Industry Parameter Standard 1 2 3 . 4 “99 Sponge Iron Plant A. Emission Standards* (Rotary Kiln) Particulate matter . Fuel Type Limiting value for concentration coal 100 mg/Nm? gas 50 mg/Nm® Carbon Monoxide coal/gas 1% (Vol/Vol) Stack Height**(minimum) coal/gas 300m Note: — * Emission shallbe normalized at 12% CO, in stack emission. ** Stack height shall be calculated as H=14.0 Q ©? where Q is emission of Sulphar Dioxide (SO, ) inkg /hr. i.e. SO, (kg/hr) Height (metre) upto 12,68 ] 12.69—33.08 2 33.09—69.06 E] 69.07—127.80 6 127.81—213.63 0 (De-dusting unit) Particulate matter Exiting unit New unit (mg/m’) 100 % Note:— (i) Stack attached to de-dusting unit shall have minimum height of 30.0 metre, (i) If, De-dusting unit is connected to After Burner Chamber (ABC), emission shall be emitted through common stack (minimun height 30.0 metre) having separate arrangements for emission monitoring for de- dusting unit. -- 3 of 4 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [Parr I—Skc. 3()] 1 2 3 4 B. Fugitive Emission Standards (Rotary Kiln/De- Particulate matter © Exiting unit New unit dusting unit) (ug/m’) 3000 2000 Note:— () the existing industry shall comply with a standard of 2000 pg/m’ after one year from the date of notification. (i) Fugitive emission shallbe monitored at a distance 10.0 metre from the source of fugitive emission as per following : Area Monitoring location Raw material handling area ‘Wagon tippler, Screen area, Transfer points, Stock bin area c. Crusher area Crushing plant,vibrating screen, transfer points Raw material feed area Feeder area, Mixing area, Transfer points Cooler discharge area Over size discharge area, Transfer points Product processing area Intermediate stock bin area, Screening plant, Magnetic separation unit, Transfer points, Over size discharge area, Product separation area, Bagging area Other areas as specified by State Pollution Control Board/Pollution Controt Committee C. Effluent Standards pH 5590 Total suspended solids 100mg/l Oil & grease 10mg/t Chemical oxygen demand 250mg/l Note:— () All efforts shall be made to reuse and re-circulate the water and to ‘maintain ‘Zero discharge’. (i) Stormwater drain shall be provided within the premises of the industry 50 as to avoid mixing with effluent.”. dated 2000, 17th dated G.SR| the 5ty [F. No. Q- 15017/95/2000-CPW (Pt.)] RK.VAISH, Jt.
